title: SimulatorConfig
description:
Scenario configuration for the simulator.
The Simulator configuration is the root of the scenario configuration.

# Example

```yaml
version: 1.6.0
max_time: 10.0
log:
  log_level: 
    type: Info
results: null
time_analysis:
  exporter:
    type: TraceEventExporter
  output_path: time_performance # .json or .csv will be appended
  analysis_unit: s
random_seed: null # Different seed each run
environment:
  map_path: null
robots:
  - name: robot1
    navigator:
      type: GoTo
      target_point: null
      target_speed: 0.5
      stop_distance: 0.2
      stop_ramp_coefficient: 0.5
    controller:
      type: PID
      robot_model:
        type: Unicycle
        wheel_distance: 0.25
      proportional_gains: [1.0, 1.0]
      derivative_gains: [0.0, 0.1]
      integral_gains: [0.0, 0.0]
    physics:
      type: Internal
      model:
        type: Unicycle
        wheel_distance: 0.25
      initial_state:
        pose: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
        velocity: [0.0, 0.0]
    state_estimator:
      type: Perfect
      prediction_activation:
        perdiod: {type: Num, value: 0.1}
      targets:
        - self
    sensor_manager:
      sensors: []
    network:
      range: 0.0 # unlimited range
      reception_delay: 0.0
computation_units: []
scenario:
  events: []
```

base_path.description: Auto-computed base directory used to resolve relative paths in the configuration.

computation_units.description: List of computation units to run, with their specific configuration.

max_time.description: Maximum simulated time before stopping the run.

random_seed.description: Optional deterministic random seed for the simulation. If not provided, a different seed will be used at each run.

robots.description: List of the robots to run, with their specific configuration.

version.description:
Version of the simulator used by the configuration. Produces a warning the the major version (second number)
is different from the current one.

AnalysisUnit.description: Unit for the time analysis results, to make a more readable report.

oneOf.description:
Documentation


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `s`, `seconds`
Serialization values(s) for this variable: `ms`, `milliseconds`
Serialization values(s) for this variable: `us`, `µs`, `microseconds`
Serialization values(s) for this variable: `ns`, `nanoseconds`

AreaEventTriggerConfig.description:
Area-based trigger configuration.

Default value: [`AreaEventTriggerConfig::Rect`] with [`RectAreaEventTriggerConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Axis-aligned rectangle trigger area.

inside.description: If `true`, trigger when inside the rectangle; otherwise when outside.

oneOf.description: Circular trigger area.

inside.description: If `true`, trigger when inside the circle; otherwise when outside.

BenchStateEstimatorConfig.description: Allow to run a list of [`StateEstimator`] outside of the simulation control loop.

name.description: Human-readable estimator name to identify the estimator in the simulation results.

BernouilliRandomVariableConfig.description: Configuration for a Bernoulli random variable.

probability.description: Success probabilities for each Bernoulli output component.

ComputationUnitConfig.description:
Configuration of the [`NodeType::ComputationUnit`].

To get observations from other nodes, specify the `send_to` option of [`ManagedSensorConfig`](crate::sensors::sensor_manager::ManagedSensorConfig).

Default values:
- `name`: `"NoName"`
- `network`: [`NetworkConfig::default`]
- `state_estimators`: empty vector
- `labels`: empty vector

# Example
```yaml
computation_units:
- name: Central Unit
  state_estimators:
  - name: central_perfect
    config: 
      type: Perfect
      prediction_activation:
        period: {type: Num, value: 0.1}
      targets:
      - robot1
      - robot2
```

labels.description: Free-form labels attached to the node metadata.

oneOf.description: Configuration for [`PID`](pid::PIDConfig).

derivative_gains.description:
Derivative gains (`Kd`) ordered by model convention.

Default for unicycle: `[0., 0.1]` (longitudinal, angular).

Default for holonomic: `[0., 0., 0.1]` (longitudinal, lateral, angular).

integral_gains.description:
Integral gains (`Ki`) ordered by model convention.

Default for unicycle: `[0., 0.]` (longitudinal, angular).

Default for holonomic: `[0., 0., 0.]` (longitudinal, lateral, angular).

proportional_gains.description:
Proportional gains (`Kp`) ordered by model convention.

Default for unicycle: `[1., 1.]` (longitudinal, angular).

Default for holonomic: `[1., 1., 1.]` (longitudinal, lateral, angular).

robot_model.description:
Robot model used to interpret gain vector sizes and ordering.

Default: `None`.

oneOf.description: Configuration for [`ExternalController`](external_controller::ExternalControllerConfig).
Configuration for [`PythonController`](python_controller::PythonControllerConfig).

DisplacementSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting displacement fault model strategies.

Default value: [`DisplacementSensorFaultModelConfig::AdditivePreDisplacement`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive fault applied in the frame of the robot before displacement.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Additive fault applied in the frame of the robot after displacement.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Python-implemented custom fault model.
Plugin-provided external fault model.

DisplacementSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting displacement sensor filtering strategies
for DisplacementSensor observations.

When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`DisplacementSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: Python-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to user-defined Python methods.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

Displacement following X axis in the frame of the robot before displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`, `dx`

Displacement following Y axis in the frame of the robot before displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`, `dy`

Rotation of the robot during the displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `rotation`, `r`

Euclidean norm of the displacement (sqrt(x^2 + y^2))


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `translation`, `t`

Displacement following X axis in the frame of the robot before displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`, `dx`

Displacement following Y axis in the frame of the robot before displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`, `dy`

Rotation of the robot during the displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `rotation`, `r`

Euclidean norm of the displacement (sqrt(x^2 + y^2))


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `translation`, `t`

Lie distance during the displacement (velocity * time)


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `distance`, `d`

Euclidean norm of the robot linear velocity during the displacement


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

DisplacementSensorVariablesProp.description: Authorized variables for additive fault proportional scaling

Displacement following X axis in the frame of the robot before displacement

EnvironmentConfig.description:
Configuration for building an [`Environment`].

The map contains the map geometry. It is loaded from a file when `map_path` is provided, or initialized as empty otherwise. The map file should be parsable by the [`Map`] struct, which currently supports a simple custom format (see its documentation for details).

The map path is relative to the simulator config path.

map_path.description:
Optional map file path.

If `None` (default), an empty [`Map`] is used.

EventConfig.description:
Configuration of a single scenario event.

An event combines a trigger condition ([`EventTriggerConfig`]) and an action
([`EventTypeConfig`]).

Default values:
- `triggering_nodes`: empty vector
- `trigger`: [`EventTriggerConfig::default`] (time trigger)
- `event_type`: [`EventTypeConfig::default`] (kill `"$0"`)

event_type.description: Action executed when the trigger condition is met.

triggering_nodes.description:
Names of the nodes that can trigger this event. If empty, any node can trigger it.
Regexp patterns are supported.
Only applied to non-time triggers.

EventTriggerConfig.description:
Trigger condition for scenario events.

Default value: [`EventTriggerConfig::Time`] with [`TimeEventTriggerConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Time-based trigger.
Distance-based trigger relative to another target.
Geometric-area trigger.

EventTypeConfig.description:
Defines the type of event to execute.

The name provided in the variants refers to the name of the nodes or use $0 notation for
dynamic referencing (e.g., the robot that triggered the event is $0).

oneOf.description: Spawns a new node according to [`SpawnEventConfig`].
Kills a node by name.

GNSSSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting GNSS fault model strategies.

Default value: [`GNSSSensorFaultModelConfig::Additive`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive fault model in robot-centered coordinates.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Clutter fault model.

apparition.description: Random variable used to sample the number of clutter observations. Should be one-dimensional and return non-negative integer values.

distributions.description: Random-variable list used to sample clutter observation values.

observation_id.description: Identifier assigned to generated clutter observations.

variable_order.description: Optional explicit mapping order from sampled dimensions to variables.

oneOf.description: Misdetection fault model.

apparition.description:
Bernoulli distribution used to decide whether an observation is detected.

This must be one-dimensional for this model.

oneOf.description: Plugin-provided external fault model.
Python-implemented fault model.

GNSSSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting GNSS sensor filtering strategies
for GNSSSensor observations.

When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`GNSSSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: Python-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to user-defined Python methods.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

InternalLog.description: Internal debug categories used when [`LogLevel::Internal`] is selected.

oneOf.description: Enable all internal categories.
Network message lifecycle logs.
Detailed network message logs.
Service request/response handling logs.
Setup phase summary logs.
Detailed setup phase logs.
Sensor manager summary logs.
Detailed sensor manager logs.
Node execution summary logs.
Detailed node execution logs.
Detailed node synchronization logs.
API bridge logs (Python and PluginAPI).
Detailed navigator computation logs.
Scenario loading and update logs.
Environment summary logs.
Detailed environment logs.
Disable all logs.
Enable only error logs.
Enable warning and error logs.
Enable info, warning, and error logs.
Enable debug, info, warning, and error logs.
Enable debug logs with additional internal category filtering.

LoggerConfig.description: Logger configuration applied at simulator startup.

excluded_nodes.description:
List of node names to exclude from logs.

Empty by default, meaning no exclusion filter.

included_nodes.description:
List of node names to include exclusively in logs.

Empty by default, meaning no inclusion filter.

ManagedSensorConfig.description:
Configuration of one managed sensor entry.

It defines the sensor instance, optional destination nodes for forwarded observations,
and whether the sensor is event-triggered. Observation that are sent to other nodes are also
available locally in the node's sensor manager, so they can be used by the node's state estimator.

Default values:
- `name`: `"some_sensor"`
- `send_to`: empty vector
- `triggered`: `false`, setting it to `true` ignore the activation times of the sensor and wait for [`SensorTriggerMessage`] to produce observations.
- `config`: [`SensorConfig::Speed`] with [`SpeedSensorConfig::default`]

name.description: Name used to identify this sensor in records and trigger channels.

send_to.description: Destination node names receiving forwarded observations from this sensor.

triggered.description: Whether this sensor produces observations only when explicitly triggered.

NavigatorConfig.description:
Enumerate the configuration of the different strategies.

The navigator computes control errors from a target behavior.

oneOf.description: Configuration for [`trajectory_follower::TrajectoryFollower`].

forward_distance.description:
Forward projection distance used during map matching, in meters.

Default: `1.0`.

stop_distance.description:
Distance threshold to force stop near the trajectory end, in meters.

Default: `0.2`.

stop_ramp_coefficient.description:
Ramp coefficient applied to remaining distance when reducing speed near the end.

Default: `0.5`, meaning that the target speed will be reduced to 0.5 times the remaining distance when 
the robot is within `stop_distance` of the end of the trajectory.

target_speed.description:
Target linear speed in m/s.

Default: `0.5`.

trajectory_path.description:
Path of the trajectory configuration file.

The file must deserialize as [`TrajectoryConfig`]. Relative paths are resolved from
the configuration path.
Default: empty string (`""`), which makes [`TrajectoryFollower::from_config`] fall back to
[`TrajectoryFollower::new`].

oneOf.description: Configuration for [`external_navigator::ExternalNavigator`].
Configuration for [`python_navigator::PythonNavigator`].
Configuration for [`go_to::GoTo`].

stop_distance.description:
Distance threshold to force stop near the target point, in meters.

Default: `0.2`.

stop_ramp_coefficient.description:
Ramp coefficient applied to remaining distance when reducing speed near the target.

Default: `0.5`.

target_point.description:
Initial target point in world coordinates `[x, y]`.

Default: `None`.

target_speed.description:
Target linear speed in m/s.

Default: `0.5`.

range.description:
Maximum communication range in meters.

Use `0.0` to disable range filtering (default: `0.0`).

reception_delay.description:
Fixed additional delay applied when receiving messages.

Use `0.0` for no additional delay (default: `0.0`).

NumberConfig.description:
Numeric configuration value.

This enum is typically used in higher-level `*Config` structures when a field can be either a
fixed number or a random variable.

Default: [`NumberConfig::Num`] with `0.0`.

oneOf.description: Deterministic numeric value.
Random variable description to sample numeric values from.

OrientedLandmarkSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting oriented-landmark fault model strategies.

Default value: [`OrientedLandmarkSensorFaultModelConfig::AdditiveRobotCentered`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive fault model in robot-centered coordinates.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Additive fault model in observation-centered coordinates.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Clutter fault model.

apparition.description: Random variable used to sample the number of clutter observations. Should be one-dimensional and return non-negative integer values.

distributions.description: Random-variable list used to sample clutter observation values.

observation_id.description: Identifier assigned to generated clutter observations.

variable_order.description: Optional explicit mapping order from sampled dimensions to variables.

oneOf.description: Misdetection fault model.

apparition.description:
Bernoulli distribution used to decide whether an observation is detected.

This must be one-dimensional for this model.

oneOf.description: Misassociation fault model.

apparition.description: Bernoulli variable controlling whether misassociation is triggered.

distribution.description: Random variable used to sample the candidate index.

sort.description: Strategy used to order candidate labels before sampling.

source.description: Source from which candidate labels are collected.

oneOf.description: Plugin-provided external fault model.
Python-implemented fault model.

OrientedLandmarkSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting among multiple sensor observation filtering strategies for oriented-landmark sensors.

This enum provides a unified interface for declaring sensor filters with different decision logic.
Each variant wraps the configuration for a specific filter type.
When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`OrientedLandmarkSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

# Config example:
```yaml
filters:
  - type: Range
    variables: [r, theta]
    max_range: [100, 1.57]
    min_range: [1., -1.57]
    inside: true
```

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object unique id (name for nodes, id for landmarks): excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.

accepted.description: Strings to accept. Can be regexp patterns.

priority_accept.description:
How to manage intersection between accepted and rejected sets.
If true, when a string is in the accepted and rejected sets, it is accepted.

rejected.description: Strings to reject. Can be regexp patterns.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object labels: excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.

accepted.description: Strings to accept. Can be regexp patterns.

priority_accept.description:
How to manage intersection between accepted and rejected sets.
If true, when a string is in the accepted and rejected sets, it is accepted.

rejected.description: Strings to reject. Can be regexp patterns.

oneOf.description: Python-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to user-defined Python methods.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

PeriodicityConfig.description:
Configuration of a periodic trigger.

It defines a period (fixed or random), an optional offset, and an optional
intra-period activation table.

offset.description:
Initial offset before the first activation.

If `None`, the offset defaults to the sampled period value.

table.description:
Optional list of activation times inside each period.

Values are interpreted as offsets within one period. Values should be positive and within the period duration. If not empty, activations are scheduled according to this table.

PhysicsConfig.description: Enumeration of the different physic implementations.

oneOf.description: Built-in Rust internal physics implementation.

faults.description: Physics fault model configurations applied after each update.

oneOf.description: External plugin-provided physics implementation.
Python-backed physics implementation.

PhysicsFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum for physics fault models.

Default value (generated by `#[config_derives]`):
[`PhysicsFaultModelConfig::AdditiveRobotCentered`] with
[`AdditiveRobotCenteredPhysicsFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive physics fault model in robot-centered coordinates.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Multiplicative factor applied to the proportional variable value.

proportional_to.description:
If some, the faults will be proportional to the robot velocity times this factor.
If none, the faults will be independant of the robot velocity.

variable_order.description: Optional explicit mapping from sampled dimensions to physics variables.

PhysicsVariables.description: Variables used by the AdditiveRobotCentered physics fault model.

oneOf.description:
X coordinate of the robot pose.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Y coordinate of the robot pose.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Orientation of the robot pose.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `orientation`, `z`

Norm of the robot linear velocity.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `velocity`, `v`

Longitudinal velocity.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `velocity_x`, `vx`

Lateral velocity.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `velocity_y`, `vy`

ProfileExporterConfig.description: Configuraition for the profiler exporter. It allows to select the type of exporter to use, and to configure it if needed.

oneOf.description: Exporter for the Chrome Trace Event format. It exports the time analysis results in a format compatible with the Chrome Trace Event format, which can be visualized with the Chrome Trace Viewer or Firefox Performance Tools.

inside.description: If `true`, trigger when distance is below threshold; otherwise above.

protected_target.description: Optional target name to protect from trigger activation.

RandomVariableTypeConfig.description: Configuration of random-variable type and parameters.

oneOf.description: No random variable
Fixed value
Uniform distribution

max.description: Maximum value of the uniform distribution.

min.description: Minimum value of the uniform distribution.

oneOf.description: Normal distribution

covariance.description: Flattened covariance matrix of the multivariate normal distribution.

mean.description: Mean of the multivariate normal distribution.

oneOf.description: Poisson distribution.

lambda.description: Lambda parameter(s) of the Poisson distribution. Must be non-negative.

oneOf.description: Exponential distribution.

lambda.description: Rate parameters (`lambda`) of the exponential distributions.

oneOf.description: Uniform angular distribution over `2*pi` with the given number of rays.
Explicit list of ray angles in degrees.
Explicit list of ray angles in radians.

ResultConfig.description: Configuration controlling result persistence and post-processing.

analyse_script.description:
Path to the Python analysis script (path from config location).
This script should have the following entry point:
```def analyse(records: list, config: dict, figure_path: str, figure_type: str, additional_param: dict|None)```
If this option is `None`, the script is not executed.

figures_path.description: Optional output directory for generated figures (path from config location).

python_params.description: Arbitrary parameters forwarded to the analysis script (additional_param in the script entry point). This can be used to forward any custom configuration to the analysis script without having to add it to the simulator configuration.

result_path.description:
Filename to save the results, in JSON format. The directory of this
file is used to save the figures if results are computed.

Path from config location.

If `None`, results are not saved on disk but can still be forwarded to the analysis script.

ResultSaveMode.description: Strategy used to save simulation results on disk.

oneOf.description: Save once when the simulation ends.
Save continuously as records are produced.
Save every `N` records.
Save every `period` seconds of simulated time.

RobotConfig.description:
Configuration of the [`NodeType::Robot`].

This configuration defines the different modules composing the robot, as well as their configuration.

Default values:
- `name`: `"NoName"`
- `navigator`: default [`go_to::GoToConfig`]
- `controller`: default [`pid::PIDConfig`]
- `physics`: default [`internal_physics::InternalPhysicConfig`]
- `state_estimator`: default [`perfect_estimator::PerfectEstimatorConfig`]
- `sensor_manager`: [`SensorManagerConfig::default`]
- `network`: [`NetworkConfig::default`]
- `state_estimator_bench`: empty vector
- `autospawn`: `true`
- `labels`: empty vector

# Example
```yaml
robots:
- name: robot2
  navigator: 
    type: TrajectoryFollower
    trajectory_path: path.yaml
    forward_distance: 0.2
    target_speed: 0.3
    stop_distance: 0.2
    stop_ramp_coefficient: 0.5
  controller: 
    type: PID
  physics: 
    type: Internal
    model: 
      type: Unicycle
      wheel_distance: 0.25
    initial_state:
      random:
        - type: Uniform
          min:
          - -5.0
          - -5.0
          - -3.1416
          max:
          - 5.0
          - 5.0
          - 3.1416
      variable_order: [x, y, orientation]
    faults: []
  state_estimator: 
    type: Perfect
    prediction_activation:
      period: {type: Num, value: 0.1}
    targets:
    - self
  sensor_manager:
    sensors:
    - name: RobotSensor
      send_to:
      - Central Unit
      config: 
        type: Robot
        detection_distance: 100.0
        activation_time:
          period: {type: Num, value: 0.1}
        faults: []
        filters:
          - type: Range
            variables: [x, y]
            min_range: [0, -1]
            max_range: [10, 1]
  network:
    range: 10.0
    reception_delay: 0.1
  state_estimator_bench: []
```

autospawn.description: If `true`, node starts in running state when created.

labels.description: Free-form labels attached to the node metadata.

name.description: Name of the robot. Will be used in the `model_name` field as well.

state_estimator_bench.description:
Additional [`StateEstimator`](crate::state_estimators::StateEstimator) to be evaluated but without a feedback
loop with the [`Navigator`](crate::navigators::Navigator)

RobotModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting the robot kinematic model.

Please note that the robot model is transfered from the Physics configuration to
other modules (e.g. controller) if it differs or is not provided.

Default value: [`RobotModelConfig::Unicycle`] with [`UnicycleConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Differential-drive unicycle model configuration.

wheel_distance.description: Distance between the two wheels, to compute the angular velocity from the wheel speeds.

oneOf.description: Holonomic model configuration.

RobotSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting robot sensor fault model strategies.

Default value: [`RobotSensorFaultModelConfig::AdditiveRobotCentered`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive fault model in robot-centered coordinates.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Additive fault model in observation-centered coordinates.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Clutter fault model.

apparition.description: Random variable used to sample the number of clutter observations. Should be one-dimensional and return non-negative integer values.

distributions.description: Random-variable list used to sample clutter observation values.

observation_id.description: Identifier assigned to generated clutter observations.

variable_order.description: Optional explicit mapping order from sampled dimensions to variables.

oneOf.description: Misdetection fault model.

apparition.description:
Bernoulli distribution used to decide whether an observation is detected.

This must be one-dimensional for this model.

oneOf.description: Misassociation fault model.

apparition.description: Bernoulli variable controlling whether misassociation is triggered.

distribution.description: Random variable used to sample the candidate index.

sort.description: Strategy used to order candidate labels before sampling.

source.description: Source from which candidate labels are collected.

oneOf.description: Python-implemented fault model.
Plugin-provided external fault model.

RobotSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting among multiple sensor observation filtering strategies for robot sensors.

This enum provides a unified interface for declaring sensor filters with different decision logic.
Each variant wraps the configuration for a specific filter type.
When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`RobotSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

# Config example:
```yaml
filters:
  - type: Range
    variables: [r, theta]
    max_range: [100, 1.57]
    min_range: [1., -1.57]
    inside: true
```

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object unique id (name for nodes, id for landmarks): excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.

accepted.description: Strings to accept. Can be regexp patterns.

priority_accept.description:
How to manage intersection between accepted and rejected sets.
If true, when a string is in the accepted and rejected sets, it is accepted.

rejected.description: Strings to reject. Can be regexp patterns.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object labels: excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.

accepted.description: Strings to accept. Can be regexp patterns.

priority_accept.description:
How to manage intersection between accepted and rejected sets.
If true, when a string is in the accepted and rejected sets, it is accepted.

rejected.description: Strings to reject. Can be regexp patterns.

oneOf.description: Python-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to user-defined Python methods.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

RobotSensorVariables.description: Variables used by robot observations, filters, and fault models.

oneOf.description:
Relative X coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Relative Y coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Relative orientation (angle) of the observed robot compared to the ego robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `orientation`, `z`

Relative distance to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `r`, `d`, `distance`

Relative bearing angle to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `theta`

Velocity of the observator.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

Absolute velocity of the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `target_velocity`

Relative X coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Relative Y coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Relative orientation (angle) of the observed robot compared to the ego robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `orientation`, `z`

Relative distance to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `r`, `d`, `distance`

Relative bearing angle to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `theta`

Relative X coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Relative Y coordinate.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Relative orientation (angle) of the observed robot compared to the ego robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `orientation`, `z`

Relative distance to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `r`, `d`, `distance`

Relative bearing angle to the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `theta`

Velocity of the observator.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

Absolute velocity of the observed robot.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `target_velocity`

ScanSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting scan-sensor fault model strategies.

Default value: [`ScanSensorFaultModelConfig::AdditiveRobotCentered`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive fault applied to all point uniformally.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Per-point additive fault applied in robot-centered frame.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Per-point additive fault applied in observation-centered frame. Orientation is defined by the angle of the point in the observation, and the additive is rotated accordingly.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Clutter generation fault model.

apparition.description: Random variable used to sample the number of clutter observations. Should be one-dimensional and return non-negative integer values.

distributions.description: Random-variable list used to sample clutter observation values.

observation_id.description: Identifier assigned to generated clutter observations.

variable_order.description: Optional explicit mapping order from sampled dimensions to variables.

oneOf.description: Global misdetection fault model.

apparition.description:
Bernoulli distribution used to decide whether an observation is detected.

This must be one-dimensional for this model.

oneOf.description: Per-point misdetection fault model.

apparition.description:
Bernoulli distribution used to decide whether an observation is detected.

This must be one-dimensional for this model.

oneOf.description: Python-implemented custom fault model.
Plugin-provided external fault model.

ScanSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting among multiple sensor observation filtering strategies for scan sensors.

This enum provides a unified interface for declaring sensor filters with different decision logic.
Each variant wraps the configuration for a specific filter type.
When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`ScanSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

# Config example:
```yaml
filters:
  - type: Range
    variables: [r, theta]
    max_range: [100, 1.57]
    min_range: [1., -1.57]
    inside: true
```

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object unique id (name for nodes, id for landmarks): excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

X position component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Y position component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Range (radius) in polar coordinates.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `r`, `radius`, `d`, `distance`

Bearing angle in polar coordinates.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `theta`, `bearing`, `angle`

Relative radial velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `radial_velocity`, `v`

Observer planar speed norm.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

oneOf.description:

Serialization values(s) for this variable: `x`

Y position component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `y`

Relative radial velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `radial_velocity`, `v`

Sensor orientation, to rotate the full point cloud.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `orientation`, `z`

ScenarioConfig.description:
Root scenario configuration.

Contains the list of event declarations evaluated by the scenario system.

The events will be published on the chanel `/simba/scenario` when they are triggered, with the content of the event record (see [`EventRecord`]).

Default values:
- `events`: empty vector

events.description: Event definitions evaluated by the scenario engine.

SensorConfig.description:
Enumerates all the possible sensors configurations.

Default value (from `#[config_derives]` generated implementation):
[`SensorConfig::OrientedLandmark`]
with [`OrientedLandmarkSensorConfig::default`](crate::sensors::oriented_landmark_sensor::OrientedLandmarkSensorConfig::default).

oneOf.description: Oriented-landmark sensor configuration.

faults.description: Fault model configurations applied after filtering.

filters.description: Filter configurations applied before fault injection.

xray.description: If true, will detect all landmarks, even if they are behind obstacles (no raycasting).

oneOf.description: Speed sensor configuration.

filters.description: Filter chain applied before fault injection.

oneOf.description: Displacement sensor configuration.

faults.description: Fault model configurations applied after filtering.

filters.description: Filter configurations applied before fault injection.

lie_movement.description: If `true`, use Lie-movement mode (currently not implemented).

oneOf.description: GNSS sensor configuration.

faults.description: Fault on the x, y positions, and on the x and y velocities

filters.description: Filter configurations applied before fault injection.

oneOf.description: Robot sensor configuration.

faults.description: Fault model configurations applied after filtering.

filters.description: Filter configurations applied before fault injection.

xray.description: If `true`, line-of-sight occlusion checks are bypassed.

oneOf.description: Scan sensor configuration.

faults.description: Fault model configuration list applied after filtering.

filters.description: Filter configuration list applied before fault injection.

height.description: Height of the sensor: will only detect objects at this height or higher.

rays.description: Ray layout used to define observation directions.

oneOf.description: External sensor configuration.

SensorManagerConfig.description:
Configuration listing all the [`SensorConfig`]s.

Default values:
- `sensors`: empty vector

Sort.description:
Ordering strategy used to pick replacement labels.

This controls how candidate labels are arranged before indexing with a sampled value.

oneOf.description: Keep the source-provided order of candidates (alphabetically).
Shuffle candidates using a deterministic random generator seeded from simulation state.
Sort candidates by increasing squared distance to the observer position.

Source.description: Source used to build the candidate label list for misassociation.

oneOf.description: Use landmark identifiers from the current map.
Use identifiers of running robots from environment metadata.

model_name.description: Name of the model/template used for spawning.

node_name.description: Name assigned to the spawned node.

SpeedSensorFaultModelConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting fault models applied to speed observations.

Default value: [`SpeedSensorFaultModelConfig::Additive`] with
[`AdditiveFaultConfig::default`].

oneOf.description: Additive perturbation fault model.

apparition.description: Bernoulli trigger deciding whether the additive fault is applied.

distributions.description: Random distributions used to sample additive perturbations.

proportional_factor.description: Optional multiplicative factor applied to the proportional value.

proportional_to.description: Optional variable used as a proportional scaling source.

variable_order.description: Ordered list of target variables receiving sampled perturbations.

oneOf.description: Python-implemented custom fault model.
Plugin-provided external fault model.

SpeedSensorFilterConfig.description:
Configuration enum selecting among multiple sensor observation filtering strategies for speed sensors.

This enum provides a unified interface for declaring sensor filters with different decision logic.
Each variant wraps the configuration for a specific filter type.
When multiple filters are applied to a sensor, all must agree to keep the observation.

Default value: [`SpeedSensorFilterConfig::Range`] with [`RangeFilterConfig::default`].

# Config example:
```yaml
filters:
  - type: Range
    variables: [r, theta]
    max_range: [100, 1.57]
    min_range: [1., -1.57]
    inside: true
```

oneOf.description: Range-based filtering on enumerated variables: excludes observations where numeric values fall outside specified bounds.

inside.description: Whether matching values should stay inside (`true`) or outside (`false`) ranges.

variables.description: Ordered list of variables to test.

oneOf.description: String pattern filtering on observed object unique id (name for nodes, id for landmarks): excludes observations matching configured regexp patterns.
Plugin-based custom filtering: delegates exclusion logic to external compiled or scripted plugins.

SpeedSensorVariables.description: Variables used by speed sensors and related fault models.

oneOf.description:
Linear velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `v`, `linear_velocity`, `linear`

Angular velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `w`, `angular_velocity`, `angular`

Norm of the linear robot velocity.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

SpeedSensorVariablesFaults.description: Variables that additive speed faults can modify.

oneOf.description:
Linear velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `v`, `linear_velocity`, `linear`

Angular velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `w`, `angular_velocity`, `angular`

SpeedSensorVariablesFilter.description: Variables that can be used by filters.

oneOf.description:
Linear velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `v`, `linear_velocity`, `linear`

Angular velocity component.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `w`, `angular_velocity`, `angular`

Norm of the linear robot velocity.


Serialization values(s) for this variable: `self_velocity`

StateConfig.description:
Configuration for [`State`] in order to load a state from the configuration.

The pose should contain 3 elements.
TODO: Make a config validation scheme.

pose.description: Position and orientation of the robot

random.description: Random initialization of the state. If `pose` is not null, random values are added to the pose and velocity according to `variable_order`.

variable_order.description: Ordered list mapping sampled random values to state variables.

StateEstimatorConfig.description:
List the possible configs, to be selected in the global config.

To select the [`StateEstimatorConfig::Perfect`], the yaml config should be:
```YAML
state_estimator:
  type: Perfect
  prediction_activation: 
    period: {type: Num, value: 0.1}
```

oneOf.description: Built-in perfect state estimator.

targets.description:
List of targets for the perfect estimator (other nodes).

"self" can be used to include the ego state in the estimation. It is included by default but
if the `targets` field is specified, it must be included explicitly to be part of the estimation.

oneOf.description: External state estimator provided through plugin API.
Python-backed state estimator.

TimeAnalysisConfig.description: Configuration for the time analysis. It allows to select the exporter to use, the output path, and the analysis unit.

analysis_unit.description: Unit for the time analysis results, to make a more readable report.

exporter.description: Exporter to use for the time analysis results export.

output_path.description:
Output path for the time analysis results. Do not add extensions, as they are added by the exporter: `.json` (for [`ProfileExporterConfig::TraceEventExporter`]) and `.report.csv`.
The path is relative to the config path

TimeEventTriggerConfig.description:
Time-based event trigger configuration.

Default values:
- `time`: `NumberConfig::Num(0.0)`
- `occurences`: `NumberConfig::Num(1.0)`

occurences.description:
Four cases:
- if `time` is a fixed number, it turns into a period and `occurences` is how many times the event will be triggered. Use 0 for infinite.
- if `time` is a random variable, `occurences` is how many samples will be drawn from it to schedule the event
- if `time` is a random variable with multiple dimensions, `occurences` is the number of repetitions of the full set of samples:
  if `time` draws N samples, and `occurences` is M, then M*N event times will be scheduled

time.description: Time specification used to schedule events.
